seasonal_wine.name: Winter dormancy and pruning, January
seasonal_wine.description: January is pruning season across the licorella terraces. With vines dormant and the schist hillsides bare, growers cut back each plant by hand on slopes too steep for mechanisation. Cellars are quiet and tasting rooms are open without harvest crowds. This is the best time to book an unhurried estate visit and to taste current-release vi ranci and aged Garnacha from barrel.
seasonal_wine.tip: Temperatures in Priorat villages drop sharply at night in January. Book accommodation with heating and check that tasting rooms are open before arriving.

seasonal_wine.name: Cellar bottling and blending decisions, February
seasonal_wine.description: February is when many producers complete barrel-to-barrel racking and begin blending decisions for the wine from the previous autumn harvest. Wines from the current vintage spend time in French and American oak, with producers tasting across barrels and selecting parcels for their top Clos bottlings. The best cellar visits in Priorat happen in February, when winemakers are in the cellar and willing to talk through their vintage decisions.
seasonal_wine.tip: A February visit to Terroir Al Limit, Mas Martinet, or Clos Mogador offers rare access to the winemaker during the blending period.

seasonal_wine.name: Budburst on the terraces, March to April
seasonal_wine.description: Garnacha and Carinena break bud in late March across the lower-altitude terraces of Gratallops and Bellmunt. Higher-altitude plots in Porrera and la Morera de Montsant follow in April. The terraced hillsides turn vivid green against the grey-black licorella slate, making this the most photogenic period for landscape visits. Winemakers are in the vineyard managing shoot positioning and monitoring frost risk on late cold nights.
seasonal_wine.tip: The road up to Clos Mogador above Gratallops passes through some of the most visually dramatic budburst scenery in Catalonia.

seasonal_wine.name: Fira del Vi de Falset, May
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, first weekend of May (sometimes extending to April 30 or early May)
seasonal_wine.description: The Fira del Vi de Falset, held annually around the first weekend of May, is the region's main public wine event. Around 60 producers from DOQ Priorat and DO Montsant pour their wines across the fairground in Falset. A separate professional day at Castell de Falset runs alongside the public tasting. For visitors, this is the easiest opportunity to taste a wide cross-section of Priorat producers in one place.
seasonal_wine.tip: Buy tickets in advance on firadelvi.org. The public Saturday and Sunday sessions sell out; the professional Monday session requires trade credentials.

seasonal_wine.name: Canopy management and green harvest, June
seasonal_wine.description: June is the last window before Priorat's intense summer heat arrives. Growers complete leaf removal to expose grapes to air circulation, and many estates carry out a green harvest, dropping surplus bunches to concentrate flavour in the fruit that remains. This is a demanding month in the vineyard. Tasting room visits remain pleasant in the early morning hours before afternoon temperatures climb above 35 degrees Celsius.
what_to_taste: Clos Erasmus vertical tastings by appointment
seasonal_wine.tip: June is the last comfortable walking-tour month before the August heat makes vineyard visits uncomfortable. Book a morning appointment.

seasonal_wine.name: Summer heat stress and cellar retreat, July to August
seasonal_wine.description: July and August bring extreme heat to Priorat, regularly exceeding 40 degrees Celsius in the valley floors around Gratallops and Bellmunt. Vines shut down photosynthesis during peak heat. Winemakers retreat to air-conditioned cellars or take summer holidays. Visitor numbers from coastal Catalonia rise, but the combination of heat and closed tastings makes this the least rewarding window for dedicated wine tourism.
what_to_taste: chilled white Priorat from Garnacha Blanca
seasonal_wine.tip: If visiting in July or August, focus on the coastal day trips to Tarragona or Sitges and plan Priorat cellar visits for September when the harvest season opens.

seasonal_wine.name: Garnacha harvest opens, early to mid September
seasonal_wine.description: Priorat's harvest typically begins with the earlier-ripening varieties in Bellmunt and Gratallops in early to mid September. Syrah and Merlot come first, followed by Garnacha across the lower-altitude terraces. Hand-picking on 30 to 50 degree slopes begins before dawn to preserve freshness. This is one of the most atmospheric periods to visit the region: pick crews are on the terraces, estates are in full activity, and the smell of fermenting grapes fills the villages.
seasonal_wine.tip: The road between Gratallops and la Vilella Baixa offers close views of harvest pickers on the terraced slopes. Most estates ask that visitors do not interrupt picking crews.

seasonal_wine.name: Festa de la Verema a l'Antiga, second Saturday September
seasonal_wine.description: The village of Poboleda hosts the Festa de la Verema a l'Antiga each second Saturday of September, recreating traditional harvest techniques. Participants pick grapes by hand with historical tools, press them in stone lagares, and taste the new juice. The festival has run annually for over a decade and draws visitors from across Catalonia who want to experience harvest in a pre-industrial context.
what_to_taste: Garnacha from Poboleda Vi de Vila
seasonal_wine.tip: The village of Poboleda is signposted from Cornudella de Montsant. Parking fills quickly by mid-morning on festival day.

seasonal_wine.name: Carinena and late-ripening harvest, late September to October
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, late September to mid October
seasonal_wine.description: Carinena is the last major variety to ripen in Priorat, often not picked until early October in higher-altitude plots around Porrera and la Morera de Montsant. The late-ripening character of Carinena on licorella produces the structural backbone and tannic grip that defines the region's Clos-style wines. Producers blending Garnacha with Carinena wait for the Carinena component before completing their harvest.
seasonal_wine.tip: Porrera is known for Cariñena-driven, mineral, structured Priorat. Cims de Porrera and Vall Llach operate tasting rooms in the village.

seasonal_wine.name: Vi Novell new wine release, autumn
seasonal_wine.description: November sees the release of vi novell, new wine from the just-completed harvest, at cooperatives and some estate producers. The Catalan equivalent of Beaujolais Nouveau, vi novell is a carbonic-maceration or minimal-intervention wine bottled weeks after picking. The Falset-Marca cooperative and Vinicola del Priorat release their vi novell for local consumption and wine bar listings in Falset. The celebration is low-key and local rather than commercial.
seasonal_wine.tip: Vi novell is available at the Falset cooperative and at wine bars on the Carrer de la Bassa in Falset for a few weeks only.

seasonal_wine.name: Library release and back-vintage tastings, October to November
seasonal_wine.description: October and November are when several Priorat producers release back-vintage library bottles and hold allocation tastings for mailing list customers and trade buyers. Estates including Clos Mogador, Mas Martinet, and Terroir Al Limit typically announce autumn releases in this window. The harvest is complete and producers are in a reflective mood, making this a productive time for serious cellar-focused visits.
what_to_taste: Mas Martinet Cami Pessebre back vintages
seasonal_wine.tip: Allocation lists for top Priorat wines fill quickly. Contact producers directly in October if you want to be placed on their mailing lists for the following year.

seasonal_wine.name: Festes de Sant Antoni Abat, 17 January
seasonal_wine.description: Sant Antoni Abat, the patron saint of animals and rural communities, is celebrated on 17 January across Priorat villages with blessings of animals, traditional dances, and communal meals accompanied by local wine. The festival is most animated in the smaller villages of Poboleda, Torroja, and la Vilella Alta. Local cooperatives and producers open bottles for village communal meals and the event has a deeply local character.
seasonal_wine.tip: Sant Antoni is a deeply local celebration. Visitors who attend respectfully and participate in communal meals will find it one of the most authentic Priorat experiences outside harvest season.
